topActions.title: Feed founder feedback directly into ranking and escalation policy
topActions.rationale: Founder feedback now carries 37.5% linkage and 38% escalation pressure; ranking should respond to that behavior directly.
topActions.firstStep: Map explicit approve/defer/reject/edit feedback signals into recommendation weighting and queue escalation posture.
topActions.successMetric: Top actions and approval pressure adapt to founder behavior instead of just reporting it.

skepticReview.counterargument: This action is well-supported but high-stakes. Confirm that the first step ("Map explicit approve/defer/reject/edit feedback signals into recommendation weig...") is achievable with current resources before approving.
assumptions: Core assumptions appear well-grounded given current evidence depth and uncertainty.
preConditions: Verify success metric: "Top actions and approval pressure adapt to founder behavior instead of just reporting it." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Supporting evidence averages 0% freshness. A refresh may overturn the recommendation.
Only 0 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 1 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Top actions and approval pressure adapt to founder behavior instead of just reporting it.

topActions.title: Turn leverage modeling into a regime-aware studio allocation engine
topActions.rationale: Leverage scoring is useful, but studio-wide decisions require a recommended split of founder, engineering, and creative attention tied to regime and dependency pressure.
topActions.firstStep: Attach explicit founder/engineering/creative allocation recommendations to the top leverage targets and summarize the best current studio-wide allocation move.
topActions.successMetric: Portfolio leverage outputs name both the project and the recommended time split for acting on it.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Portfolio leverage outputs name both the project and the recommended time split for acting on it.

topActions.title: Push dashboard and hub summaries onto the operator rendering spine
topActions.rationale: Startup already inherits operator posture; dashboard and hub views should stop maintaining parallel summary logic before drift reappears.
topActions.firstStep: Route dashboard and hub-facing summaries through the operator command center so posture, trust movement, and unlock status share one runtime source.
topActions.successMetric: Dashboard, startup, founder, and hub-facing outputs all describe the same evidence gaps, trust shifts, and unlock posture.

skepticReview.counterargument: This action is well-supported but high-stakes. Confirm that the first step ("Route dashboard and hub-facing summaries through the operator command center so ...") is achievable with current resources before approving.
assumptions: Core assumptions appear well-grounded given current evidence depth and uncertainty.
preConditions: Verify success metric: "Dashboard, startup, founder, and hub-facing outputs all describe the same evidence gaps, trust shifts, and unlock posture." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Supporting evidence averages 0% freshness. A refresh may overturn the recommendation.
Only 0 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 2 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Dashboard, startup, founder, and hub-facing outputs all describe the same evidence gaps, trust shifts, and unlock posture.

topActions.title: Expand evidence provenance and contradiction tracing
topActions.rationale: Evidence density is 3.55 with 0% stale nodes; provenance and contradiction edges should drive trust explicitly.
topActions.firstStep: Persist provenance metadata, contradiction edges, and citation-like source links in the evidence graph.
topActions.successMetric: Every major recommendation can show evidence depth, freshness, and contradiction pressure.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Every major recommendation can show evidence depth, freshness, and contradiction pressure.

topActions.title: Harden shared external feed validation
topActions.rationale: The external feed seam now carries decision-grade adapters, so manifest-level validation and downgrade rules need to be stricter than generic feed-valid/feed-invalid tagging.
topActions.firstStep: Add adapter manifests, required-signal validation, and invalid-feed downgrade receipts for each high-risk external adapter.
topActions.successMetric: Signal coverage receipts explain why an adapter is trusted, invalid, or still shadow-only instead of showing generic feed status only.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Signal coverage receipts explain why an adapter is trusted, invalid, or still shadow-only instead of showing generic feed status only.

topActions.title: Deepen outcome learning and regret tracking
topActions.rationale: Recommendation quality should improve from measured results, not narrative confidence; hit rate is 100% and regret is 0.25.
topActions.firstStep: Convert open hypotheses into intervention IDs with baselines, horizons, and later-session observations, then let regret directly demote weak strategies and actions.
topActions.successMetric: Top actions show improving hit rate, stronger experiment coverage, and decreasing regret over time.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Top actions show improving hit rate, stronger experiment coverage, and decreasing regret over time.

topActions.title: Increase external evidence connector coverage
topActions.rationale: Coverage is 65%; higher autonomy is unjustified until external evidence replaces local proxies.
topActions.firstStep: Add real GitHub/review/deploy/proposal/founder-override evidence sources and rank them by expected decision lift.
topActions.successMetric: Signal coverage exceeds 90% and the top missing adapters are no longer core governance inputs.

skepticReview.counterargument: This action is well-supported but high-stakes. Confirm that the first step ("Add real GitHub/review/deploy/proposal/founder-override evidence sources and ran...") is achievable with current resources before approving.
assumptions: Assumes 69% evidence depth is sufficient to act; a deeper evidence pass may reverse this recommendation.
preConditions: Significant effort (50%) required — confirm scope is achievable before approving.
Verify success metric: "Signal coverage exceeds 90% and the top missing adapters are no longer core governance inputs." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Supporting evidence averages 0% freshness. A refresh may overturn the recommendation.
Only 0 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 1 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Signal coverage exceeds 90% and the top missing adapters are no longer core governance inputs.

topActions.title: Grade top recommendations against do, delay, and opposite counterfactuals
topActions.rationale: Recommendation authority remains soft until IGNIS compares the selected path against plausible alternatives and resolves those differences over time.
topActions.firstStep: Attach compact do/delay/opposite scenarios to the top actions so later outcomes can grade whether the chosen path was actually superior.
topActions.successMetric: Top actions carry explicit alternative-path expectations that can be checked in later sessions.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Top actions carry explicit alternative-path expectations that can be checked in later sessions.

topActions.title: Replace global execution gating with adapter-specific unlock ladders
topActions.rationale: Execution honesty improves when deploy, review, hub, and social adapters each expose their own blocked/shadow/dry-run/live-ready state instead of one global gate.
topActions.firstStep: Compute unlock stage per high-risk adapter from trust, validation state, and source class; surface the next unlock condition everywhere.
topActions.successMetric: Execution posture names which adapters are blocked, which are shadow-only, and which are ready for dry-run or live work.

skepticReview.counterargument: This action is well-supported but high-stakes. Confirm that the first step ("Compute unlock stage per high-risk adapter from trust, validation state, and sou...") is achievable with current resources before approving.
assumptions: Core assumptions appear well-grounded given current evidence depth and uncertainty.
preConditions: Verify success metric: "Execution posture names which adapters are blocked, which are shadow-only, and which are ready for dry-run or live work." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Supporting evidence averages 0% freshness. A refresh may overturn the recommendation.
Only 0 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 2 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Execution posture names which adapters are blocked, which are shadow-only, and which are ready for dry-run or live work.

topActions.title: Repair the weakest pillar: creativity
topActions.rationale: The current bottleneck is creativity; a live plan should concentrate effort where score lift is most constrained.
topActions.firstStep: Trace the lowest-confidence factors under creativity and convert them into explicit evidence-backed work items.
topActions.successMetric: creativity improves without weakening the strongest pillar on the next two score cycles.

skepticReview.counterargument: Evidence depth is only 62%. The recommendation may be correct, but the supporting evidence is thin. A single contradicting data point could reverse the ranking.
assumptions: Assumes 62% evidence depth is sufficient to act; a deeper evidence pass may reverse this recommendation.
preConditions: Verify success metric: "creativity improves without weakening the strongest pillar on the next two score cycles." is measurable before proceeding.

adversaryFindings.summary: Only 1 evidence node(s) back this recommendation across 1 ref(s); a single new contradicting signal could flip the ranking.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: creativity improves without weakening the strongest pillar on the next two score cycles.

topActions.title: Evolve personality into an uncertainty-aware policy layer
topActions.rationale: Personality should be earned from regime, evidence quality, and outcome learning instead of sounding dynamic while staying static underneath.
topActions.firstStep: Let regime, contradiction pressure, and confidence budget directly control assertiveness and narrative mode.
topActions.successMetric: Tone and certainty now move with evidence quality, hit rate, and contradiction load.

experimentContract.expectedMetric: Tone and certainty now move with evidence quality, hit rate, and contradiction load.

topActions.title: Upgrade execution from canned queue to dynamic plan execution
topActions.rationale: The winning strategy is growth-max; execution should operate on the live top actions rather than a fixed queue.
topActions.firstStep: Generate guarded actions from the current action plan, including command hints, rollback notes, and verification criteria.
topActions.successMetric: Queue items correspond to live top actions and record observed outcomes after execution.

entries.summary: Use VITALITY momentum to expand studio reach
actions: Ship action intelligence and write-capable MCP tools
entries.rationale: VITALITY is carrying enough momentum that controlled expansion can compound current gains.

entries.summary: Stop adding pillars and make decisions measurable
entries.rationale: IGNIS already has enough systems; the leverage now comes from proving which ones actually work.

entries.summary: Stabilize truth fidelity before expanding scope
actions: Turn contradictions into first-class blocking signals
entries.rationale: The downside of adding more surface area before truth fidelity is stronger is still high.

actions: Expose concise executive summaries and approvals
entries.rationale: Founder usability is the bottleneck between current intelligence and real studio leverage.

entries.summary: Reduce model overreach and firm up CREATIVITY
actions: Instrument calibration before major scoring changes
entries.rationale: CREATIVITY remains fragile enough that uncalibrated expansion could outrun truth quality.

consensus: Ship action intelligence and write-capable MCP tools
